exercises.title: Library Evaluation Framework
exercises.description: Create a comprehensive evaluation framework for PQC libraries.
tasks: Define evaluation criteria categories (security, maintenance, features)
Document findings in a comparison table
exercises.expectedOutput: Library evaluation report with scoring and recommendations
hints: Consider: audit status, constant-time claims, CVE history
Weigh security criteria more heavily than features
Check documentation quality and example code
Look at issue response times and release frequency

exercises.title: Implementation Checklist Creator
exercises.description: Build a tool that generates implementation checklists for PQC projects.
exercises.starterCode:
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import List
from enum import Enum

class Priority(Enum):
    CRITICAL = 1
    HIGH = 2
    MEDIUM = 3
    LOW = 4

@dataclass
class ChecklistItem:
    id: str
    category: str
    description: str
    priority: Priority
    algorithm_specific: List[str] = None  # None means applies to all

def create_pqc_checklist() -> List[ChecklistItem]:
    """
    Create comprehensive PQC implementation checklist.
    """
    items = [
        # Security checks
        ChecklistItem(
            id="SEC-001",
            category="Security",
            description="Verify library is using constant-time operations",
            priority=Priority.CRITICAL
        ),
        ChecklistItem(
            id="SEC-002",
            category="Security",
            description="Implement secure memory zeroization",
            priority=Priority.CRITICAL
        ),
        ChecklistItem(
            id="SEC-003",
            category="Security",
            description="Validate random number generator quality",
            priority=Priority.CRITICAL
        ),
        
        # Testing checks
        ChecklistItem(
            id="TEST-001",
            category="Testing",
            description="Run NIST Known Answer Tests (KAT)",
            priority=Priority.CRITICAL
        ),
        ChecklistItem(
            id="TEST-002",
            category="Testing",
            description="Implement fuzz testing for inputs",
            priority=Priority.HIGH
        ),
        
        # TODO: Add more items for:
        # - Key management
        # - Algorithm-specific checks (FN-DSA floating point, etc)
        # - Deployment checks
        # - Documentation requirements
    ]
    
    return items

def generate_markdown(items: List[ChecklistItem]) -> str:
    """
    Generate Markdown formatted checklist.
    """
    output = "# PQC Implementation Checklist\n\n"
    
    categories = set(item.category for item in items)
    
    for category in sorted(categories):
        output += f"## {category}\n\n"
        category_items = [i for i in items if i.category == category]
        
        for item in sorted(category_items, key=lambda x: x.priority.value):
            priority_icon = {
                Priority.CRITICAL: "🔴",
                Priority.HIGH: "🟠",
                Priority.MEDIUM: "🟡",
                Priority.LOW: "🟢"
            }[item.priority]
            
            output += f"- [ ] {priority_icon} **{item.id}**: {item.description}\n"
        
        output += "\n"
    
    return output

# Generate and print checklist
checklist = create_pqc_checklist()
print(generate_markdown(checklist))
exercises.expectedOutput: Working checklist generator with comprehensive items
hints: Include both general and algorithm-specific items
Add references to standards where applicable

exercises.title: Security Requirements Document
exercises.description: Write a security requirements document for a PQC implementation project.
tasks: Define the scope and threat model
hints: Start with threat model (who are attackers, what do they want)

exercises.title: Implementation Risk Assessment
exercises.description: Perform a risk assessment comparing different implementation approaches.
tasks: Identify risks for reference, optimized, and constant-time implementations
Estimate likelihood and impact for each risk
hints: Consider both security and operational risks
Map risks to specific implementation choices
